descript.abstract: This dataset is intended for read-only use. This dataset represents the transactional boundaries of areas (excluding National Wilderness and National Wild & Scenic Rivers) which impose management or use restrictions on National Forest System (NFS) lands. In this dataset, discrete polygons represent the original area designated and subsequent additions/deletions/modifications to that area. These are areas designated by a Public Law, Executive Order, Presidential Proclamation, or other authority. Related details include the date of designation, status of the final boundary description, authority, and land status case and document information associated with the areas.The boundaries in this dataset provide detailed information suitable for advanced land status mapping and analysis. The OtherNationalDesignatedArea dataset provides the current area boundaries (not split by transaction) which is suitable for most mapping and analysis. The purpose of these data is to provide display, identification, and analysis tools for determining current boundary information for Forest Service managers, GIS specialists, and others.
descript.purpose: This dataset represents the transactional boundaries of areas (excluding National Wilderness and National Wild & Scenic Rivers) which impose management or use restrictions on National Forest System (NFS) lands. It also provides detailed information about the designation of the area, such as the designation date, legal authority, establishing document, and more. These areas were designated by legal authority above the Agency level (i.e. Congress and/or the President). Examples of other national designated areas include National Recreation Area, National Monument, and National Game Refuge.

idinfo.useconst: The USDA Forest Service makes no warranty, expressed or implied, including the warranties of merchantability and fitness for a particular purpose, nor assumes any legal liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness or utility of these geospatial data, or for the improper or incorrect use of these geospatial data. These geospatial data and related maps or graphics are not legal documents and are not intended to be used as such. The data and maps may not be used to determine title, ownership, legal descriptions or boundaries, legal jurisdiction, or restrictions that may be in place on either public or private land. Natural hazards may or may not be depicted on the data and maps, and land users should exercise due caution. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible to verify the limitations of the geospatial data and to use the data accordingly.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

cntinfo.cntvoice: Please send an e-mail to the address below.

attracc.attraccr: The Regional Land Status personnel are responsible for their local data stewardship and maintenance. The overall collection nationwide consists of 100% attribute accuracy based on currentness and completeness.

dataqual.logic: Quality control measures taken: this feature class is populated by an automated translation process from the source feature class that is edited and maintained by the data stewards. Only stewards with the proper role grants can edit the data. The application used for editing has built in controls to only allow valid geometry and domains to control attribute integrity.
dataqual.complete: This theme is current to date of publication.

horizpa.horizpar: Generally the source information used to record spatial locations are from: 1) on-screen digitizing with a georeferenced topographic or image background; or 2) GPS field collected positions.

procstep.procdesc: This data was published using Feature Manipulation Engine Software. The spatial features were extracted from the transactional database, manipulated to meet the desired output format and published to the target feature class. This data was migrated from the CDW. The spatial features were projected and imported into the target feature class.

enttyp.enttypd: A collection of geographic features with the same geometry type (such as point, line, or polygon), the same attributes, and the same spatial reference.

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: A unique control number used to identify a specific spatial feature and/or database attribute.
The official name given to the case
The local Case identification code assigned

attr.attrdefs: U.S. Forest Service ALP Reference Documentation: OTHER_NATIONAL_AREA_TYPES

edom.edomv: WILD AND SCENIC RIVER OTHER AREA
edom.edomvd: WILD AND SCENIC RIVER OTHER AREA

edom.edomv: WILD AND SCENIC RIVER STUDY AREA
edom.edomvd: WILD AND SCENIC RIVER STUDY AREA

attr.attrdef: The Area Name assigned to each Other Nationally Designated Area
Code associated with nationally designated area

attrdomv.udom: Internal identifier code generated from the legacy ALP LSI system, or indication of administration by another federal agency

attr.attrdef: Legal status of the Regulated Use boundary.
Date area was officially established (may differ from the Authority date)
The official acres defined the original authority.

attrdomv.udom: The area of a feature in acres.

attr.attrdef: The area of the feature in acres, calculated using a projected surface computation. A NAD 83, USA Contiguous Albers Equal Area Conic projection was used for Regions 1-9, and NAD 83, Alaska Albers for Region 10.

attrdomv.udom: The calculated area of a feature in acres.

attr.attrdef: An unique serial number identifier assigned by the Bureau of Land Management.
Foreign key to corresponding non-Status Publication featureclass

U.S. Forest Service Region, consisting of Region 01 (Northern Region), Region 02 (Rocky Mountain Region), Region 03 (Southwestern Region), Region 04 (Intermountain Region), Region 05 (Pacific Southwest Region), Region 06 (Pacific Northwest Region), Region 08 (Southern Region), Region 09 (Eastern Region), and Region 10 (Alaska Region). There is no Region 07. Regions are listed on https://www.fs.usda.gov/organization. Within this dataset, the region represents the Land Status Record System Production Regional schema from which data was derived.
Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

cntorgp.cntorg: USFS Chief Information Office, Enterprise Data Warehouse

distinfo.distliab: The U.S. Forest Service makes no warranty, express or implied, nor assumes any liability or responsibility for the accuracy, reliability, completeness, or utility of these geospatial data or for the improper or incorrect use of those data. The data are dynamic and may change over time. The user is responsible for verifying the limitations of the geospatial data and for using the data accordingly.
